chosen_architecture:
HYBRID — a strict-Diátaxis SSOT backbone (Candidate 1) carrying a FastAPI-style progressive Tutorial spine as its Tutorials arm (Candidate 2's spine), with Candidate 2/3's verb-and-goal routing grafted in as NAVIGATION ONLY (links/cards, never duplicated content). Top-level nav is the four Diátaxis estates + a "Start here" router + an executable Examples gallery + a walled-off Contributing estate.

Why it won the panel: the three judging lenses split cleanly and the hybrid is the only design that takes each lens's winner where that lens is decisive. (1) MAINTAINABILITY/SSOT lens scored Candidate 1 highest (9) because it is the ONLY candidate that makes single-source-of-truth a STRUCTURAL invariant, not an editorial convention: "Explanation owns the why, Reference owns the signature/parameters, neither restates the other — the only permitted relationship is a hyperlink", plus a mechanical, topic-agnostic placement rule ("which mode is the reader's posture?") that tells a future contributor where a NEW page goes without judgment. It also DELETES the duplicating front-door pages outright (getting_started/concepts, persona_paths, onboarding/) rather than re-linking them, permanently removing drift sources. This directly dissolves audit #6 (3–4 competing front doors, concepts taught 3–4×) and #18 (api/↔reference duplication) by construction. So Diátaxis is the backbone. (2) NEWCOMER-ONBOARDING lens scored Candidate 1 only 6, because four equal mode cards echo the current confusing 6-card grid and strict purity fragments a topic across four pages — the opposite of what a novice wants. Candidate 1's own #1 weakness. The fix the judges converged on: graft Candidate 2's single linear Tutorial rail ("one primary CTA per on-ramp page"; Lesson 1 = swap synthetic data for your own folder; the second task is structurally pre-decided as "Next →") as the Tutorials ARM of the Diátaxis backbone, so onboarding gets the FastAPI rail while the IA keeps Diátaxis governance. (3) FIT-TO-SURFACE lens scored Candidate 2 highest (9) because making the runnable examples/*.py the LITERAL gallery (the examples ARE the integration tests) is the tightest possible fit to nirs4all's reality, and a linear spine keeps the library's inherently-unified mechanisms (branching/merging, generators) WHOLE in one lesson each — whereas strict one-page-per-mode FRACTURES them. The ENFORCEABILITY lens additionally crowned Candidate 2's IA-agnostic gate suite (literalinclude-from-tested-examples; the must-call-run() check that is the ONLY plan to close defect #32; the dedicated broken-example-ref gate for the persona_paths #8 dead-path class) while crowning Candidate 1 for the one precise inline-lint detail (match assign-then-pass-positionally, not "pipeline = [", verified: only 3 truly-compliant run(pipeline=[ sites in docs vs 655 candidate-grep hits, with writing_pipelines.md:1242's list-of-pipelines as the real false positive).

The winner therefore = Candidate 1's four-mode SSOT skeleton + placement law as the spine; Candidate 2's progressive Tutorial rail + executable-gallery + IA-agnostic gate suite as the Tutorials arm and the safety net; Candidate 2/3's verb/goal routing demoted from top-level content tracks to a small "I already have a goal →" router block on the Start-here page and bottom-of-page "Next goals →" links (navigation, not a fifth set of pages — which is what kept Candidate 3 at 6 on maintainability, since 7 re-walking task tracks structurally re-invite the audit's concept duplication keyed by goal). Candidate 3's genuinely best idea — generate Reference AND the gallery FROM SOURCE/DISK (autosummary-recursive; sphinx-gallery over examples/*.py) — is adopted as the zero-drift IMPLEMENTATION of the Reference and Examples estates, because generated-not-handwritten is the only true zero-drift mechanism in the exercise.
rationale: Grounded entirely in audit §8's four mandates. (1) "One canonical learning path, one source of truth per concept" → Diátaxis backbone with the structural SSOT law (Explanation owns why, Reference owns signatures, they only link) and the mechanical placement rule for new pages — this is the durable governance the audit demands, not a one-off cleanup. (2) "Style mandate becomes infrastructure" → the precise assign-then-pass inline lint with a STYLE.md-driven allowlist. (3) "Documentation must enter the safety net" → literalinclude-from-tested-examples + per-PR example subset + must-call-run() gate + tightened nitpick + CI/RTD strictness reconciliation. (4) "Metadata sourced from one place" → conf.py reads nirs4all.__version__, license/citation templated from LICENSE with a string-match (not CeCILL-banning) grep gate. The hybrid resolves the central panel tension — Candidate 1 wins maintainability but loses onboarding; Candidate 2 wins onboarding+fit but introduces a teach-vs-explain duplication; Candidate 3 wins anti-drift generation but its 7 tasks re-create duplication — by keeping Diátaxis's structural SSOT as the law, embedding the FastAPI rail as the Tutorials arm (so the Tutorial is the single canonical learning estate, not a second concept home: it TEACHES with runnable steps and links sideways to the ONE Explanation page, never restating the concept body), and using generation for the drift-prone estates. Verb/goal needs (retrain/explain/predict/session each findable) are met by navigation cards into Tutorial lessons, How-to recipes, and Reference pages — never by duplicated tracks.
target_nav_tree:
Start here (index.md — thin router, ONE "Start here → first run" CTA + small "I already have a goal →" links; teaches nothing)
├─ Get Started / first run (install + zero-data generate.regression() inline run() + single "Continue to the Tutorial →")
└─ I already have a goal → (nav links into Tutorial lessons, How-to recipes, Reference — no content)

Tutorial (learning-oriented; the single linear FastAPI rail; persistent prev/next; one new concept per lesson; every step a runnable inline run(); each step links sideways "Why it works →" Explanation + "Run this yourself →" Gallery)
├─ L1 Your first model (swap synthetic for your own folder path)
├─ L2 Preprocessing (SNV — one new concept)
├─ L3 Datasets & loading
├─ L4 Cross-validation
├─ L5 Models & scoring/refit
├─ L6 Reading results & predict()
├─ L7 Classification
├─ L8 Visualization
├─ L9 Hyperparameter tuning (generators)
├─ L10 Branching & merging
├─ L11 Augmentation
├─ L12 Export & deploy (.n4a, predict reuse)
└─ L13 Retrain & transfer (retrain mode=full|transfer|finetune)

How-to guides (task-oriented recipes; goal-titled; self-contained; NO concept-teaching — link to Explanation; NO "why")
├─ Data: loading_data, sample_filtering, signal_types, aggregation(retitled), synthetic_data
├─ Preprocessing: snv, resampler, handbook, cheatsheet, transfer_preprocessing_selector_cheatsheet
├─ Models: training, hyperparameter_tuning, deep_learning, tabpfn_nirs
├─ Pipelines: branching, merging, stacking, multi_source, cache_optimization, generators(how-to slice)
├─ Augmentation: sample_augmentation_guide, synthetic_nirs_generator
├─ Predictions: making_predictions, advanced_predictions, exporting_models, analyzing_results, session_api
├─ Deployment: export_bundles, prediction_model_reuse, retrain_transfer
├─ Visualization: shap, prediction_charts, in_pipeline_charts, pipeline_diagram
├─ Operations: logging
└─ Troubleshooting: faq, dataset_troubleshooting

Reference (information-oriented; generated where possible; ONE home — api/ dissolved into it)
├─ Syntax & keywords: pipeline_syntax, pipeline_keywords, generator_keywords (ONE canonical; combination_generator links to it)
├─ Operator catalog: operator_catalog, transforms, models, splitters, filters, augmentations
├─ Public API (run/predict/explain/retrain/session/generate + RunResult/PredictResult/ExplainResult): curated contract pages
├─ reference/api/ (357 autosummary-recursive stubs, mounted UNDER reference, one tree)
├─ Configuration: configuration/index, cache_config
├─ Storage & workspace: storage (merged ex-api/storage), workspace (merged ex-api/workspace)
├─ Session reference (ex reference/api/session.md, wired in)
├─ Metrics
└─ CLI

Explanation (understanding-oriented; the SINGLE canonical concept home; read not run; links DOWN to Reference, never restates a signature)
├─ pipelines (absorbs onboarding/pipeline_workflow + getting_started/concepts residue)
├─ datasets (absorbs onboarding/data_workflow)
├─ cross_validation
├─ branching_and_merging
├─ generators
├─ augmentation
├─ predictions_and_deployment (absorbs scoring_and_refit, understanding_predictions conceptual half)
├─ mental_models / overview (absorbs onboarding/mental_models)
└─ workspace & reproducibility (from workspace_intro + storage rationale; NOT the schema)

Examples gallery (executable; sphinx-gallery over examples/*.py; tagged by Tutorial lesson / How-to recipe; counts derived from disk)
├─ User track (U) — surfaced gallery, mapped to lessons/recipes
├─ Reference track (R) — wired to Reference pages (R01–R03,R05–R07 + new R04_visualization)
└─ (Developer D track lives under Contributing)

Contributing & internals (separate audience; never competes as a front door; internally split by the same four modes)
├─ Architecture: architecture, pipeline_architecture, outputs_vs_artifacts
├─ Extending: controllers (+ onboarding/controllers_intro), custom operators
├─ Internals: caching, artifacts, artifacts_internals, metadata, synthetic
├─ Testing & dev gallery (D-track examples)
├─ Documentation style (STYLE.md — the inline mandate + allowlist)
└─ Migration: duckdb_to_sqlite, storage_migration_webapp, troubleshooting/migration

About (metadata, all source-derived): changelog, license (dual AGPL-3.0-or-later default), citation, roadmap pointer

Non-nav machine file: llms.txt (ex ai_onboarding.md — removed from human toctree, regenerated from canonical anchors, metadata-from-source)
front_door: ONE canonical path, no competing choice. The current FOUR front doors (getting_started/, concepts/, onboarding/, ai_onboarding.md) + the 6-card landing grid (audit #6) collapse to: index.md becomes a THIN ROUTER that teaches nothing — a single prominent "Start here → first run" CTA, four secondary Diátaxis mode cards + one Examples card, and a small "I already have a goal →" link block (the only surviving trace of persona routing, as navigation not content). The CTA leads to ONE Get-Started page whose ONLY forward link is "Continue to the Tutorial →" (Candidate 2's "one primary CTA per on-ramp page" rule). That page: `pip install nirs4all`, then the literal first copy-paste — `result = nirs4all.run(pipeline=[MinMaxScaler(), ShuffleSplit(n_splits=3), {"model": PLSRegression(n_components=10)}], dataset=nirs4all.generate.regression(n_samples=500))` then `print(result.best_rmse)` — which succeeds with ZERO user data because the dataset is synthetic (the audit's #1 onboarding blocker removed; this snippet is literalinclude-tied to a CI-run example so it cannot be wrong like result.predict() #3 was). Then Tutorial L1 changes exactly ONE thing — swap synthetic for the user's own folder path — and the prev/next rail makes every "second task" structurally pre-decided. Concrete deletions that make the single door real: getting_started/concepts.md DELETED (residue → Explanation); persona_paths.md DELETED (its fake load_dataset() #7 and 5 broken refs #8 die with it); onboarding/ DISSOLVED into Explanation + Contributing; ai_onboarding.md removed from the human toctree → llms.txt machine file. Net: exactly one Start-here, exactly one conceptual home (Explanation), zero front-door overlap.
grafted_ideas: From Candidate 1 (maintainability winner): the STRUCTURAL SSOT law adopted verbatim as the governing rule — 'Explanation owns the why, Reference owns the signature/parameters, neither restates the other; the only permitted relationship is a hyperlink.' This is the single most drift-proof sentence in the panel.

From Candidate 1: the mechanical, topic-agnostic placement rule for NEW pages ('which mode is the reader's posture? learning→Tutorial, doing-a-task→How-to, looking-up→Reference, understanding→Explanation') as a CONTRIBUTING checklist — converts a one-off cleanup into durable governance.

From Candidate 1: DELETE the duplicating front-door pages (getting_started/concepts, persona_paths, onboarding/) rather than re-link them — deletion removes a drift source permanently; re-linking (C2/C3) leaves a second copy alive to drift.

From Candidate 1: the precise inline lint — match 'pipeline-named var assigned a list literal then passed POSITIONALLY to run()', NOT 'the word pipeline on a line', recognizing compliant multi-line inline calls (verified: writing_pipelines.md:1242 run(pipeline=[pipeline_pls,...]) is a real false positive; only 3 truly-compliant run(pipeline=[ in docs).
From Candidate 1: name BOTH halves of the CI/RTD strictness contradiction (docs.yml -W --keep-going vs .readthedocs.yaml fail_on_warning:false) and reconcile to the same strictness.

From Candidate 2 (onboarding + fit winner): the single linear Tutorial rail as the Tutorials arm — 'one primary CTA per on-ramp page', the only forward link is 'Continue to the Tutorial →', each lesson's primary action is 'Next →', so the second task is structurally pre-decided not menu-chosen.
From Candidate 2: Lesson 1 = swap the synthetic dataset for the user's OWN folder, still one inline run() — the lowest-friction bridge from zero-data success to a real first run.

From Candidate 2: LITERALINCLUDE-FROM-TESTED-EXAMPLES as the primary doc-snippet mechanism — the rendered snippet IS a slice of a CI-run examples/*.py file, so doc/example drift is impossible BY CONSTRUCTION (defeats #3/#4/#9/#12/#14 at the source, sidesteps the myst_nb fixture burden).

From Candidate 2: the must-call-run() example gate (the ONLY plan to close defect #32 — verified D01_metadata_branching.py and D03_repetition_transform.py have 0 run() calls) and the dedicated broken-example-ref gate for the persona_paths/U21-U22 dead-path class (#8/#15) that doc-execution cannot catch.
From Candidate 2: STYLE.md is BOTH the human style page AND the machine-readable allowlist the lint reads — exceptions are auditable and 'cannot silently widen.'

From Candidate 3 (anti-drift generation): generate Reference (autosummary-recursive over the 357 symbols) AND the gallery (sphinx-gallery over examples/*.py) FROM SOURCE/DISK — counts, U05/U06 (#17), R04 (#34), and new public symbols self-heal; generated-not-handwritten is the only true zero-drift mechanism.

From Candidate 3: realize verb/goal/persona routing as NAVIGATION (cards + 'Next goals →' links) that point at the ONE canonical page many ways, never as duplicated content tracks — gives findability without re-creating the audit's concept duplication.

From all three (consensus, must-keep): convert the stale 1.8MB health-1/5 examples/predictions.ipynb into a runnable examples/reference/R04_visualization.py on the public PredictionAnalyzer API — fills the R04 gap (#34) and kills the un-CI'd broken notebook (#14) in one move.

From all three: the api/ tree is DELETED not maintained beside reference/ — 4 hand-written pages merge into their reference/ twins (storage ~794→404, workspace 125→208), 357 stubs mount UNDER reference/api/ as one tree, generator syntax (#38) collapses to ONE canonical page; 'two trees documenting one surface is structurally impossible afterward' is an explicit acceptance criterion.

Diátaxis mode badge on every Tutorial lesson / How-to recipe page (Candidate 2's idea) — grafts Candidate 1's orientation clarity (reader always knows learning vs looking-up vs understanding) onto the friendlier non-quadrant nav.
style_guide:
THE RULE: every code block that runs a pipeline writes it INLINE in one shot inside run(), with no external variable: `result = nirs4all.run(pipeline=[ ...inline steps... ], dataset=...)`. The anti-pattern, forbidden, is `pipeline = [...]` assigned to a variable then `nirs4all.run(pipeline, ds)`. Stated ONCE in Contributing → Documentation style (STYLE.md), shown by example on the very first Get-Started snippet, then enforced mechanically and never re-explained per page.

THE CANONICAL EXAMPLE (the literal first copy-paste, GROUND_TRUTH-verified, doctest/literalinclude-backed): `result = nirs4all.run(pipeline=[MinMaxScaler(), ShuffleSplit(n_splits=3), {"model": PLSRegression(n_components=10)}], dataset=nirs4all.generate.regression(n_samples=500))`.

THE DOCUMENTED EXCEPTIONS (machine-readable in STYLE.md, which IS the allowlist the lint consumes, plus per-site `# noqa: inline-pipeline` markers with a reason — so the carve-out is auditable and cannot silently widen): (a) deliberate REUSE/COMPARISON scripts that run the SAME pipeline through several run()/PipelineRunner/Session calls or build a list-of-pipelines (a handful in 06_deployment/06_internals; AND the verified false positive writing_pipelines.md:1242 `run(pipeline=[pipeline_pls, pipeline_rf, pipeline_nn])`); (b) synthesis/utility scripts that are not pipeline tutorials (developer/02_generators/D05–D09, examples/_internal/*); (c) compliant inline calls merely SPLIT ACROSS MULTIPLE PHYSICAL LINES (which a naive grep false-positives on — Codex's 605-vs-655 note).

THE CI LINT (the exact pattern to flag): a ruff custom rule or dedicated scripts/ grep over docs/source/**.md fenced python blocks AND examples/**.py that flags ONLY the precise anti-pattern — a statement-level assignment whose target name contains `pipeline` and whose value is a LIST LITERAL, where that same name is later passed POSITIONALLY as the first arg to run()/PipelineRunner(). It MUST NOT flag (i) `pipeline=[` keyword args (the compliant inline form), (ii) `run(pipeline=[a, b, c])` list-of-pipelines, (iii) multi-line inline calls. Verified scope: the naive `pipeline-named var = [` grep yields ~648–655 candidate sites across 140 files but only 3 truly-compliant `run(pipeline=[` exist in docs — so precision here is load-bearing or the gate floods false positives and gets disabled. Order: ONE mechanical sweep clears the 648 BEFORE the gate turns on, so CI starts green; thereafter the gate prevents regrowth (without it 648 → 700 next release, audit §3).
examples_plan: REORGANIZED TAXONOMY (U/D/R survive only as on-disk prefixes + audience badges; users navigate by lesson/recipe/reference, never by 'U vs D'). GALLERY, not prose: the ~80 examples/*.py are the single source of truth (they already double as integration tests) and are rendered into a sphinx-gallery/literalinclude gallery generated FROM the scripts — the 8 hand-written docs/source/examples/*.md wrapper pages (which drifted: stale counts #16, undocumented U05/U06 #17, SklearnWrapper #12) are DELETED and regenerated. RELATION TO DOCS (embed, not duplicate): each Tutorial lesson links 'Run this yourself →' to its backing U-file; each How-to recipe links to the script that demonstrates it; each Reference page links to its canonical R-example. Because the rendered snippet IS the CI-run file, the documented-vs-tested gap is structurally closed. TRACK MAPPING: U → Tutorial/How-to gallery (01_getting_started → Tutorial; 02_data_handling → How-to/Data; 03_preprocessing → How-to/Preprocessing+Augmentation; 04_models → How-to/Models; 05_cross_validation → How-to/CV; 06_deployment → How-to/Deployment; 07_explainability → How-to/Visualization). D → Contributing & internals gallery. R → wired to Reference pages as canonical-syntax proofs (R01→pipeline_syntax, R02→generator reference, R03→keywords). R04 FIX (#34/#14): convert the stale 1.8MB health-1/5 examples/predictions.ipynb into a runnable examples/reference/R04_visualization.py using the public PredictionAnalyzer API — fills the missing-R04 gap (verified: R01–R03, R05–R07 exist, no R04) and deletes the broken un-CI'd notebook in one move. WEEKLY→PER-PR CI (#19): replace examples.yml's Sunday cron (verified `0 0 * * 0`) with a fast tagged U-subset (01_getting_started + one canonical example per section, ~7–10 files) on every push/PR; full ~80-file U+D+R suite stays nightly/weekly, so no gallery script sits broken on main for 7 days. PLUS the must-call-run() gate: every examples/**.py either calls run()/PipelineRunner/Session or carries an explicit `# syntax-only` allowlist marker — closing defect #32 (verified D01_metadata_branching, D03_repetition_transform have 0 run() calls). DOCTEST OF DOC SNIPPETS: any doc code block that cannot be a literalinclude slice (rare) is executed via myst_nb/doctest in CI with tiny synthetic datasets (generate.regression) and plots_visible=False fixtures. INVENTORY: one programmatic manifest derived from disk feeds run.sh, run_ci_examples.sh, README, and the gallery generator, so the count is stamped ONCE everywhere (#16) and undocumented examples self-surface.
reference_plan: ONE Reference home; api/ is DELETED, not maintained beside reference/ (audit #18/§5.2; explicit acceptance criterion: 'two trees documenting one surface is structurally impossible afterward'). UNIFICATION: (1) reference/ is the single canonical Reference estate and the only Reference entry in top-level nav. (2) AUTODOC STRATEGY: replace the 357 hand-touched api/*.rst stubs with an autosummary-recursive root mounted UNDER reference/api/, so adding a public symbol updates the reference with ZERO manual edits (Candidate 3's zero-drift mechanism) — a reader lands on a curated contract page and drills into autodoc only for exhaustive member listings. (3) The 4 hand-written api/*.md pages are merged into their reference/ twins then deleted: api/storage.md (~794 LOC, carries 'Version 5.0', legacy runs/ framing) → reference/storage.md (~404), dropping the duplicate; api/workspace.md (125) → reference/workspace.md (208), dropping the 100%-duplicate PipelineLibrary section; api/module_api.md → folded into the curated public-API pages, FIXING predict() first param source→model (#22); api/sklearn_integration.md → reference/ sklearn page. (4) reference/api/session.md wired into reference/index (fixes orphan #26). (5) Generator syntax — today triplicated across generator_keywords/combination_generator/pipeline_syntax with drift (#38) — collapses to ONE canonical generator-keywords reference page the others link to. (6) CURATED CONTRACT PAGES for the stable public surface (run/predict/explain/retrain/session/generate + RunResult/PredictResult/ExplainResult) own narrative + signatures; run() reference completed with the 4 missing params refit/cache/project/report_naming (#23); cli.md drops the `- runs/` line that contradicts workspace design (#24). METADATA-FROM-SOURCE: pipeline_syntax.md parameter tables generated/validated against source; its 'Dec 2025 / Phase 3' footer removed (#2). DOCSTRING FIXES AT SOURCE so autodoc stops inheriting stale calls: Predictions.load() in visualization/predictions.py:76 → from_parquet/from_file/from_workspace (#14). SSOT LAW: Reference owns parameters/schemas; Explanation links to Reference for exactness and never restates a signature.
safety_net_plan: FIVE CI gates, all per-PR, converting the entire 'ships silently' class to build failures (root cause audit §5.3: 'nothing that executes documentation exists'; verified absent today — no myst_nb/doctest/sphinx_gallery in conf.py, examples.yml schedule-only, release='0.7.1', nitpick_ignore_regex live). (1) DOC-SNIPPET EXECUTION via LITERALINCLUDE-FROM-TESTED-EXAMPLES (primary) — the rendered snippet is a slice of a CI-run examples/*.py, so doc/example drift is impossible by construction (defeats result.predict() #3, bundle= #4, plot_predictions() #9, SklearnWrapper #12, Predictions.load() #14); myst_nb/doctest with tiny generate.regression() fixtures + plots_visible=False reserved only for snippets that genuinely cannot be a whole runnable file. (2) PER-PR EXAMPLES (#19): move examples.yml off the verified Sunday cron `0 0 * * 0` to a fast tagged U+R subset on every push/PR (full U+D+R stays nightly), PLUS the MUST-CALL-run() gate — every examples/**.py either calls run()/PipelineRunner/Session or carries `# syntax-only`, closing defect #32 (verified D01_metadata_branching, D03_repetition_transform have 0 run() calls; a plain 'runs without error' check would pass them silently). (3) NITPICK TIGHTENING (§5.3A): remove the blanket nitpick_ignore_regex masking nirs4all.{data,pipeline,api}.* and the myst.xref_missing/ref.* suppressions so a typo'd :class: ref FAILS the build; reconcile the verified contradiction — docs.yml builds -W --keep-going (strict) while .readthedocs.yaml sets fail_on_warning:false — to the SAME strictness (RTD → fail_on_warning:true). Expect a backlog of real broken refs to burn down before strict can land. (4) METADATA-FROM-SOURCE + grep gates: conf.py release read from nirs4all.__version__ (verified currently 0.7.1, real 0.9.1 #2), copyright 2025→2026, license/citation templated from LICENSE in ONE place; a CI grep asserts the rendered license STRING matches the LICENSE dual-license statement (default AGPL-3.0-or-later) WITHOUT banning the legitimate CeCILL token (audit #1, GROUND_TRUTH:5), and fails if any version string != __version__. (5) PRECISE INLINE-STYLE LINT + BROKEN-EXAMPLE-REF GATE + EXAMPLE-COUNT STAMP: the assign-then-pass-positionally lint reading its allowlist from STYLE.md; a grep asserting every examples/*.py path referenced in docs exists on disk (catches persona_paths #8, U21/U22 #15, export_bundles dead refs — a class doc-execution can't catch because a wrong PATH in prose isn't executable); and the programmatic example-count must equal the harvested file count (#16).

execution_roadmap.phase: P0 — Quick wins: metadata + broken-API triage (one-commit class)
execution_roadmap.goal: Stop misleading newcomers in the first 10 minutes; clear all CRITICAL release blockers before any structural work. Pure correctness, no IA migration.
tasks: Fix license on first-contact pages to the dual-license statement (index.md:284, ai_onboarding.md:14, root CLAUDE.md:3) matching README:430-431 — default AGPL-3.0-or-later (#1)

Source conf.py release from nirs4all.__version__ (fix 0.7.1→0.9.1), bump copyright 2025→2026, fix landing/citation/ai_onboarding versions (0.2.1/0.8.6/0.9.0), drop pipeline_syntax.md 'Dec 2025/Phase 3' footer (#2)

~15-item wrong-API triage: result.predict()→nirs4all.predict() (#3), bundle=→model= (#4), load_dataset() removal (#7), persona_paths 5 broken refs (#8), PredictionAnalyzer phantom methods + pass result.predictions (#9), phantom operator class names (#10), faq y_column→target_column in global_params (#11), SklearnWrapper→NIRSPipeline (#12), examples.synthetic→nirs4all.synthesis (#13), Predictions.load() docstring at source (#14), U21/U22 refs (#15)
Convert examples/predictions.ipynb → examples/reference/R04_visualization.py on public PredictionAnalyzer API; delete the notebook (#14/#34)
Fix transfer-learning import paths D01/D03 so the feature actually demos (#20)

execution_roadmap.phase: P1 — Style sweep + inline lint (the 648 defect)
execution_roadmap.goal: Clear the inline-pipeline violations mechanically and lock them out so they cannot regrow.
tasks: One mechanical sweep over docs/source/**.md + examples/**.py converting pipeline=[...] external-var pattern to inline run(pipeline=[...]) (the propagated template defect, ~648 sites/140 files)

Author STYLE.md = human style page + machine-readable allowlist with the 3 exception classes (reuse/comparison incl. writing_pipelines.md:1242 list-of-pipelines; synthesis/utility D05–D09 + _internal; multi-line inline)

Implement the precise assign-then-pass-positionally lint (NOT 'pipeline = ['); verify it flags 0 false positives on the 3 allowlisted classes and the 3 truly-compliant run(pipeline=[ sites; turn the gate on (CI now green)
execution_roadmap.effort: 2–3 days (sweep is fast; lint precision is the work)
execution_roadmap.depends_on: P0 (don't sweep pages that are about to be deleted — coordinate the persona_paths/onboarding deletions with P3)

execution_roadmap.phase: P2 — Safety-net CI (make it stay correct)
execution_roadmap.goal: Bring documentation into the safety net so the rewrite stays correct; turn the whole 'ships silently' class into build failures.
tasks: Add sphinx-gallery + literalinclude-from-tested-examples; wire a fixture harness (tiny generate.regression datasets, plots_visible=False)
Move examples.yml off the Sunday cron to per-PR fast U+R subset; add the must-call-run()-or-#syntax-only gate (closes #32); full suite nightly
Add the broken-example-ref grep gate (#8/#15) and the disk-derived example-count stamp feeding run.sh/run_ci_examples.sh/README/gallery (#16)
Add metadata grep gates (version==__version__; license string matches LICENSE, CeCILL token NOT banned)
Reconcile docs.yml -W --keep-going with .readthedocs.yaml fail_on_warning:false (→ true) — but stage AFTER P4 nitpick burn-down
execution_roadmap.effort: 4–6 days (fixtures + gallery tooling is real engineering)
execution_roadmap.depends_on: P0 (R04 exists, APIs correct), P1 (lint pattern reusable)

execution_roadmap.phase: P3 — Structural IA migration: front doors + Explanation + Tutorial spine
execution_roadmap.goal: Execute the design — collapse 4 front doors to 1, build the single Explanation concept home and the linear Tutorial rail, delete duplicating pages.
tasks: Rewrite index.md → thin router (one 'Start here' CTA + mode cards + 'I already have a goal →' nav)

Build Get-Started page (zero-data inline run, single 'Continue to Tutorial →') + the 13-lesson Tutorial rail (one new concept/lesson, sideways 'Why it works →' + 'Run this yourself →' links, mode badges)

Establish Explanation as the single concept home: move onboarding/{mental_models,data_workflow,pipeline_workflow,workspace_intro} + scoring_and_refit + conceptual halves into concepts/*; DELETE getting_started/concepts.md, persona_paths.md, dissolve onboarding/
Re-home user_guide/** into How-to (strip embedded concepts out to Explanation); move developer/** + controllers_intro into Contributing; ai_onboarding.md → non-nav llms.txt
Repoint ALL cross-links (grep separately for direct refs, :class: refs, string-literal paths, toctree entries, re-exports per NO-SEMANTIC-SEARCH rule); add a link-check gate
execution_roadmap.effort: 1.5–2 weeks (119 pages re-homed; do in ≤5-file phases via sub-agent swarm)
execution_roadmap.depends_on: P0, P1 (clean pages move cleanly), P2 (link-check + per-PR gates catch migration breakage)

execution_roadmap.goal: Collapse api/↔reference into one generated home; eliminate the duplicate trees; then go strict.
tasks: Merge api/storage→reference/storage, api/workspace→reference/workspace, api/module_api + sklearn_integration into curated pages (fix predict() source→model #22); delete api/*.md
Replace 357 hand stubs with autosummary-recursive under reference/api/; wire session.md (#26); collapse generator syntax to one canonical page (#38)
Complete run() reference (refit/cache/project/report_naming #23); fix cli runs/ (#24); add missing imports (#27); remove PredictionResultsList (#25); document U05/U06 (#17)
Remove the blanket nitpick_ignore_regex; burn down the surfaced real broken refs; flip RTD fail_on_warning:true and confirm CI/RTD parity
Move Roadmap/Phase leakage to docs/_internal (#33); retitle aggregation.md (#31); clear LOW items (#35–#40)
execution_roadmap.depends_on: P2 (autodoc/gallery tooling), P3 (IA stable so merges land in final homes)

success_metrics: 0 inline-style violations: the precise assign-then-pass lint reports 0 across docs/source/**.md + examples/**.py (down from ~648/140 files), gate is green and enforced per-PR.
0 stale-version tokens: every rendered version string == nirs4all.__version__ (0.9.1); conf.py release sourced from __version__; metadata grep gate green.

License string on landing/citation/About matches the LICENSE dual-license statement (default AGPL-3.0-or-later); grep gate green; CeCILL token still permitted as a valid optional variant (NOT banned).

0 wrong-API references: all ~15 copy-paste-fatal defects (#3,#4,#7–#15) fixed and held by doc-snippet execution; result.predict()/bundle=/load_dataset()/plot_predictions()/SklearnWrapper/Predictions.load() absent from docs.

Exactly ONE front door: index.md is a thin router; getting_started/concepts.md, persona_paths.md, onboarding/ deleted/dissolved; ai_onboarding.md out of human toctree; 0 competing 'start here' paths (audit #6 resolved).

ONE Reference home: api/*.md deleted, 357 stubs mounted under reference/api/ via autosummary-recursive, session.md wired in; 'two trees document one surface' structurally impossible (acceptance criterion met, #18).
All doc code snippets execute in CI (literalinclude-from-tested-examples + targeted doctest); a wrong-API edit fails the build.

Per-PR example execution live: examples.yml off the Sunday cron; fast U+R subset + must-call-run() gate green; 0 examples with 0 run() calls and no #syntax-only marker (#32 closed); R04 exists, notebook gone.
Example count derived programmatically and identical across run.sh/run_ci_examples.sh/README/gallery (#16); U05/U06 documented (#17).
Nitpick strict: blanket nitpick_ignore_regex removed, RTD fail_on_warning:true == CI -W; 0 broken cross-refs; CI/RTD strictness reconciled (#19/§5.3A).
Mean segment health ≥ 4/5 (up from the audited 2.80), with no segment below 3 (landing/getting_started/onboarding/visualization lifted off 2).
